member.name: T:ContractExecuteTransaction
member.summary:
Call a function of the given smart contract instance, giving it parameters as its inputs.

It can use the given amount of gas, and any unspent gas will be refunded to the paying account.

If this function stores information, it is charged gas to store it. There is a fee in hbars to maintain that storage until the expiration time, and that fee is added as part of the transaction fee.

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.#ctor(DictionaryLinked{TransactionId,DictionaryLinked{AccountId,Proto.Transaction}})
member.param: Compound list of transaction id's list of (AccountId, Transaction) records
member.exception: when there is an issue with the protobuf

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.RequireNotFrozen
member.summary:
A maximum limit to the amount of gas to use for this call.

The network SHALL charge the greater of the following, but SHALL NOT charge more than the value of this field.

  1. The actual gas consumed by the smart contract call.
  2. `80%` of this value.
The `80%` factor encourages reasonable estimation, while allowing for some overage to ensure successful execution. The long to be set as gas {@code this}

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.RequireNotFrozen_2
member.summary: Sets the number of hbars sent with this function call.

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.RequireNotFrozen_3
member.summary: Sets the contract instance to call.

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.RequireNotFrozen_4
member.summary:
Sets the function parameters as their raw bytes.

Use this instead of {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} if you have already pre-encoded a solidity function call. This MUST contain The application binary interface (ABI) encoding of the function call per the Ethereum contract ABI standard, giving the function signature and arguments being passed to the function.

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.SetFunction(System.String)
member.summary:
Sets the function name to call.

The function will be called with no parameters. Use {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} to call a function with parameters.

The String to be set as the function name {@code this}

member.name: M:ContractExecuteTransaction.SetFunction(System.String,ContractFunctionParameters @)
member.summary: Sets the function to call, and the parameters to pass to the function.
member.param: The String to be set as the function name
The function parameters to be set
